author: Chris Schommer
title: Paul Sewald slams door for another save in win over Reds
desc: Paul Sewald struck out one over one scoreless inning to earn the save in a win over the Reds.
impact: Sewald continues a strong stretch out of the bullpen, holding opponents scoreless in nine of his last 10 trips to the mound. The right-hander remains one of the more reliable closing options for fantasy managers, posting a 3.20 ERA and 0.71 WHIP with 16 saves across 28 appearances.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Eduardo Rodriguez struggles with control in short outing against Reds
desc: Eduardo Rodriguez allowed one earned run on two hits and five walks while striking out three over 2 2/3 innings in Friday's win over the Reds.
impact: Rodriguez continues a steady stretch on the mound of late, allowing two earned runs or less in four of his last five starts. The left-hander remains one of the more stable starting options in the Diamondbacks rotation for manager Torrey Lovullo, carrying a 2.55 ERA and 1.23 WHIP with a 5-2 record and 1.9 K/BB ratio across 14 starts.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Nick Lodolo steady on mound in no-decision
desc: Nick Lodolo allowed two earned runs on five hits and one walk while striking out five over 5 1/3 innings in Friday's loss to the Diamondbacks.
impact: Lodolo has struggled to miss opposing bats, allowing three earned runs or more in five of seven trips to the mound. The left-hander remains a high-risk starting option in the Reds rotation for manager Terry Francona, posting a 5.21 ERA and 1.45 WHIP with a 2-1 record and 7.1 K/9 ratio through seven starts this season.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Louis Varland locks down another save in win over Yankees
desc: Louis Varland struck out two batters over one scoreless innings to earn the save in Friday's 8-5 win over the Yankees.
impact: Varland continues a strong stretch out of the bullpen, holding opponents scoreless in eight of his last nine trips to the mound. The right-hander continues to reward manager John Schneider with an elite closing option, carrying a 0.49 ERA and 0.95 WHIP with 12 saves and five holds across 33 appearances.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Trey Yesavage survives wild start as Blue Jays outslug Yankees
desc: Trey Yesavage allowed five earned runs on four hits and six walks while striking out three over five innings in Friday's win over the Yankees.
impact: Yesavage has struggled to miss opposing bats of late, allowing five earned runs or more in three of his last four starts. The right-hander remains one of the more inconsistent options in the Blue Jays rotation for manager John Schneider, posting a 3.78 ERA and 1.26 WHIP with a 3-3 record and 1.9 K/BB ratio across nine starts.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Spencer Torkelson homers in loss to Guardians
desc: Spencer Torkelson went 1-for-3 in Friday's 3-2 loss to the Guardians with a solo home run.
impact: Torkelson continues a productive stretch at the plate, driving in runs in four of his last eight contests. The first baseman remains one of many underperforming in a disappointing Tigers lineup, batting .213 with 11 homers, 28 RBI, and 25 runs scored through 69 games.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Jordan Lawlar swipes two bases in 5-2 win over Reds
desc: Jordan Lawlar went 2-for-4 in a win over the Reds with one walk, one run scored, two RBI, and two stolen bases.
impact: Lawlar was impactful in his first game action since April 2, coming through with his bat and his legs in the win. The 23-year-old will look to provide manager Torrey Lovullo another dynamic bat in the Diamondbacks lineup, batting .364 with one home run, four RBI, four runs scored, and three stolen bases across seven games.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Gabriel Moreno chips in with double and steal in 5-2 win
desc: Gabriel Moreno went 1-for-4 in win over the Reds with a double, a run scored, one stolen base, and one walk.
impact: Moreno has heated up at the plate, registering hits in four of his last five games. The catcher offers manager Torrey Lovullo another stable option in the Diamondbacks lineup, slashing .260 with five home runs, 22 RBI, 23 runs scored, and four stolen bases through 45 games.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Geraldo Perdomo chips in a hit and a steal in win
desc: Geraldo Perdomo went 1-for-5 in a win over the Reds with one run scored and one stolen base.
impact: Perdomo continues a solid stretch at the plate of late, recording hits in eight of his last 11 contests. The infielder remains one of the more streaky options in the Diamondbacks lineup, batting .240 with three home runs, nine steals, 29 runs scored, and 22 RBI across 66 games.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Corbin Carroll chips in steal in win over Reds
desc: Corbin Carroll went 1-for-4 in a win over the Reds with one walk and one stolen base.
impact: Carroll has heated back up at the plate, picking up hits in four of his last five contests. The outfielder remains one of the more impactful options in the Diamondbacks lineup for manager Torrey Lovullo, carrying a .915 OPS with 11 home runs, 33 RBI, 42 runs scored and eight stolen bases through 66 games.

author: Ari Koslow
title: Justin Wrobleski (hamstring) expected to start Tuesday
desc: Justin Wrobleski is on track to start against the Rays on Tuesday.
impact: Wrobleski left his previous start early with a hamstring contusion. It appears he'll be good to go without requiring missing a start.

author: Ari Koslow
title: Daulton Varsho (wrist) to resume baseball activities next week
desc: Daulton Varsho is expected to resume baseball activities next week.
impact: Varsho received a cortisone injection to address a lingering left wrist issue. He is expected to return to on-field activities within the next few days.

author: Ari Koslow
title: Evan Carter (oblique) placed on IL
desc: The Rangers placed OF Evan Carter on the 10-day injured list with a right oblique strain.
impact: Carter suffered the injury in Friday's series opener. Cody Freeman was recalled as the corresponding move.

author: Ari Koslow
title: Cody Freeman recalled from Triple-A
desc: The Rangers recalled INF/OF Cody Freeman from Triple-A Round Rock.
impact: Freeman will add another option off the bench with Evan Carter headed to the injured list.

author: Ari Koslow
title: Corey Seager (jaw/rib) not in lineup Saturday
desc: Corey Seager is not in the Rangers lineup on Saturday against the Red Sox.
impact: Seager remains day-to-day. Nicky Lopez draws the start in his place.
